questions.prompt: What is the core problem Printix is built to solve for an MSP customer?
choices: Encrypting documents end-to-end with a customer-managed key
Replacing the on-prem print server with a cloud service plus a workstation agent
Providing managed multifunction printer hardware
Acting as a web filter that blocks confidential documents from being printed
questions.explanation: Printix is cloud-native print management. The point is to retire the Windows print server VM, push driver and queue management into the cloud, and run printing through a Printix Client agent on each workstation.

questions.prompt: Printix sells and ships its own printer hardware that customers must use to take advantage of the service.
questions.explanation: Printix is software-only. It works with the customer's existing supported printers and MFPs across Brother, Canon, Epson, Fujifilm, HP, Konica Minolta, Kyocera, Lexmark, Ricoh, Xerox, and others.

questions.prompt: By default, where does the encrypted print data live between submission and release?
choices: On Printix-managed storage in the Printix Cloud
On the user's computer, via the Printix Client
On the on-prem print server
On the printer's internal hard drive
questions.explanation: The Printix Client stores encrypted job data locally on the user's computer. The customer can opt to enable their own Azure Blob Storage or Google Cloud Storage to break that local-machine coupling.

questions.prompt: Which of the following are top-level Administrator pages a frontline tech should know how to find? (Select all that apply.)
choices: Dashboard
Printers
Computers
Authentication
Web Filtering Policies
questions.explanation: Dashboard, Printers, Computers, and Authentication all live in the Administrator menu. Web Filtering Policies is a DNSFilter concept, not Printix.

questions.prompt: Printix automatically assigns a three-letter ID (e.g. ASD, BNM) to every registered printer to make it easier for users to identify the right one.
questions.explanation: The three-letter printer ID is auto-assigned at registration and printed on the printer's ID sign and QR code, so a user can scan to find the right queue.

questions.prompt: Which of these are valid ways for a printer to end up on a user's computer in a Printix tenant? (Select all that apply.)
choices: User opens the Printix Client menu, picks Printers, selects the printer, and clicks Add
User scans the printer's QR code with the Printix App
Administrator adds a print queue and configures Add print queue automatically with the user's group
User downloads the driver from the manufacturer's website and installs it manually
Technician copies the driver via USB and runs the printer install wizard
questions.explanation: Self-service via Printix Client, QR/NFC discovery via the Printix App, and group-based automatic deployment are all documented Printix paths. Manufacturer-website and USB-stick installs are pre-Printix workflows that Printix exists to retire.

questions.prompt: A user can't see a printer in their Printix Client. Which of these are common causes? (Select all that apply.)
choices: The user is not in the group required for that print queue's Exclusive access
The user's computer is on a network that doesn't have access to that printer
The print queue is marked inactive (administrator-only)
The user's web browser doesn't support WebGL
The user's MFA token has expired
questions.explanation: The first three are real Printix visibility filters: group membership, network match, and active flag. WebGL and MFA-token expiry don't gate Printix Client printer-list visibility.

questions.prompt: What's a real trade-off of using Printix Anywhere instead of Print Later to a specific printer?
choices: Anywhere requires Microsoft Entra Conditional Access to be disabled
Anywhere uses a virtual queue so the user only picks the release device at the printer; the matching device-specific driver isn't selected up front
Anywhere stops working after 12 hours
Anywhere disables the Save-O-Meter dashboard counter
questions.explanation: Print Later targets a specific printer with its specific driver, so device-specific behaviour (trays, finishing) is locked in at submit time. Anywhere is a virtual queue: the user picks the release device when they walk up, which is the trade-off vs guaranteeing a specific driver was used.

questions.prompt: Printix Go and the Printix App are two names for the same software.
questions.explanation: The Printix App runs on a phone, tablet, or browser and lets the user release jobs remotely. Printix Go is software that installs on the printer's touchscreen so the user can authenticate and release at the device with no phone.

questions.prompt: A user reports they sent a job and nothing printed. Put these triage checks in the most useful order.
items: Confirm the user can sign in to the Printix Client
Confirm the user's computer is online in the Administrator's Computers page
Confirm the user can see the right print queue
Confirm the job reached the printer (printer's History tab)
questions.explanation: Each check gates the next. Sign-in, then agent online, then queue visibility, then job delivery.

questions.prompt: A user gets the message 'Your sign-in was successful but does not meet the criteria to access this resource.' What's the most likely root cause?
choices: Their Printix Client is out of date
The customer's Microsoft Entra Conditional Access policy is blocking Printix for this user or group
Their printer is offline
The Printix tenant is in a different region
questions.explanation: That exact message is documented as a Microsoft Entra Conditional Access denial. Frontline scope is to recognise it and escalate; the change is in the customer's Entra policy, not in Printix.

questions.prompt: If a user closes their laptop and walks to the printer, a Print Later job stored on the laptop can fail to release because the source computer is offline.
questions.explanation: Default storage is the user's computer. If the source is offline at release time, Printix can't deliver the job. Customer-managed cloud storage (Azure Blob, Google Cloud) is the documented fix.
